$comment: THE UX-PARITY GATE CONTRACT. A Godot screen that replaces a React screen must show the SAME
INFORMATION IN THE SAME WORDS (migration decision: option A — faithful reproduction).
Each entry names the React panel being replaced and lists the i18n keys that panel RENDERS.
The gate resolves each key against godot/data/i18n-ja.json (the same ja.ts React reads), then
asserts the text actually appears in the built Godot screen. Interpolated keys ({gold}) are
requiredKeys were derived by reading each React panel's t("...") call sites — they are not a
wish list, they are what the shipped React screen puts on screen.
`evidence` is the comparison screenshot the milestone cannot be declared done without.
AGENTS.md is the authority this encodes: 'Do not call town services done if they are just
lists. Shops must show who can use equipment and what changes. Recovery must show cost,

screens.notes: Per-wounded row must show hp/maxHp -> maxHp (before/after) and that member's cost; healthy members are NOT listed. town.cannotAffordRecovery must appear when the purse is short.

AGENTS.md: a shop MUST show who can use the equipment (town.canEquip/cannotEquip against the selected adventurer) and what changes (town.equipmentSummary stat deltas). A name+price list is a gate failure.

Appraisal is PAID (loot.appraiseCost badge, loot.appraiseCantAfford when broke). Bulk convert has a CONFIRM/CANCEL stage (loot.confirmDismantle/confirmSell). An appraised piece shows a fit comparison vs the selected member and an equip-on-them action.
Must name the stat each reinforce boosts (workshop.boosts), the materials cost, and the at-cap / cant-afford states (workshop.atCap, workshop.cantAfford).
Each quest must show its objective PROGRESS (questBoard.progress) and reward breakdown (rewardGold/rewardXp), plus repeatable / claimedTimes state.

Two-pane: pinned selected adventurer (career.overview/current/mastery) beside the scrolling destination list grouped advanced|foundations. EVERY destination renders its signature, stat shifts, equip slots and granted techniques — this was the exact remediation that fixed 'too thin to decide in'.
The bestiary is the decision surface for preparation: threat, weaknesses and drops per known enemy, not a name list.
